meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drowning in tutorials. Learn the lean analytics stack for Founders using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate your MVP and drive revenue fast.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start measuring. By implementing a lightweight analytics stack via Antigravity, you transform your MVP into a data-driven revenue engine. Stop building features in the dark; use event-based tracking to validate user behavior and iterate toward product-market fit with absolute precision.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks watching YouTube tutorials, stacking framework upon framework, yet your app remains a ghost town. You are trapped in 'Tutorial Hell,' a phantom productivity cycle where you feel busy but ship nothing of value. The market doesn't care about your clean code or complex architecture; it cares about solving problems they are willing to pay for.

To escape, you must stop being a student and start being a founder. We are shifting your focus from 'learning to build' to 'building to measure.' By integrating a lean analytics layer today, you gain the ability to see exactly where your users drop off, allowing you to prioritize the features that actually move the revenue needle.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single user action that correlates with revenue. This is your primary event.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my SaaS business model and suggest 3 high-impact user events I should track to validate product-market fit for an MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my business plan and define the primary North Star metric for tracking user success.'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Layer
steps.description: Inject a lightweight, privacy-first analytics provider into your existing codebase without bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a lightweight analytics integration module for my Next.js project using PostHog or Plausible that handles event batching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize analytics tracking module with event batching and error handling.'

steps.title: Implement Event Hooks
steps.description: Place strategic tracking hooks at critical conversion points: Signup, Paywall View, and Checkout Success.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable React hook 'useTrackEvent' that wraps my analytics provider and adds metadata automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the checkout flow to include useTrackEvent hooks for purchase success.'

steps.title: Automate Data Pipelines
steps.description: Ensure your data flows into a simple dashboard, so you don't have to manually check the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to automate the export of daily active users and conversion rates to a Slack webhook.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a cron job that pushes daily conversion metrics to Slack via webhook.'

steps.title: Review and Iterate
steps.description: Use the data to kill unused features and double down on what users actually engage with.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the past 7 days of event logs to identify the top 3 features with the highest drop-off rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Based on the logs in /analytics, which features are causing the highest churn?'

pro_tips: Never track everything; track only the events that correlate to a paid subscription or long-term retention.
Place your analytics script at the top of the <head> tag, but defer execution to keep your Lighthouse score above 90.
Use distinct user IDs from your database rather than generic session cookies to track long-term user journeys.
Set up an automated alert for 'Zero Revenue Days' so you know exactly when to pivot your marketing strategy.
If your event volume is low, use raw SQL queries on your DB instead of expensive third-party analytics tools.
content.next_steps: Now that you have visibility, spend 48 hours observing user paths. If users aren't hitting your North Star metric, simplify your UI or adjust your value proposition immediately.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping products that people actually pay for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
